items.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
items.description: Today, we're previewing the fastest way to run Ollama on Apple silicon, powered by MLX, Apple's machine learning framework.

items.title: The simplest and fastest way to setup OpenClaw
items.description: Setup OpenClaw in under two minutes with a single Ollama command.

items.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
items.description: Ollama now supports subagents and web search in Claude Code.

items.title: OpenClaw
items.description: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant that connects your messaging apps to local AI coding agents, all running on your own device.

items.title: ollama launch
items.description: ollama launch is a new command which sets up and runs coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex with local or cloud models. No environment variables or config files needed.

items.title: Image generation (experimental)
items.description: Generate images locally with Ollama on macOS. Windows and Linux support coming soon.

items.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
items.description: Ollama is now compatible with the Anthropic Messages API, making it possible to use tools like Claude Code with open models.

items.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
items.description: Open models can be used with OpenAI's Codex CLI through Ollama. Codex can read, modify, and execute code in your working directory using models such as gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b, or other open-weight alternatives.

items.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
items.description: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to bring the latest gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models to users for safety classification tasks. gpt-oss-safeguard models are available in two sizes: 20B and 120B, and are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

items.title: MiniMax M2
items.description: MiniMax M2 is now available on Ollama's cloud. It's a model built for coding and agentic workflows.

items.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark performance
items.description: We ran performance tests on release day firmware and an updated Ollama version to see how Ollama performs.

items.title: New coding models & integrations
items.description: GLM-4.6 and Qwen3-coder-480B are available on Ollama’s cloud service with easy integrations to the tools you are familiar with. Qwen3-Coder-30B has been updated for faster, more reliable tool calling in Ollama’s new engine.

items.title: Qwen3-VL
items.description: Ollama now supports Alibaba's Qwen3-VL.

items.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark
items.description: The latest NVIDIA DGX Spark is here! Ollama has partnered with NVIDIA to ensure it runs fast and efficiently out-of-the-box.

items.title: Web search
items.description: A new web search API is now available in Ollama. Ollama provides a generous free tier of web searches for individuals to use, and higher rate limits are available via Ollama’s cloud.

items.title: New model scheduling
items.description: Ollama now includes a significantly improved model scheduling system, reducing crashes due to out of memory issues, maximizing GPU utilization and performance, especially on multi-GPU systems.

items.title: Cloud models
items.description: Cloud models are now in preview, letting you run larger models with fast, datacenter-grade hardware. You can keep using your local tools while running larger models that wouldn’t fit on a personal computer.

items.title: OpenAI gpt-oss
items.description: Ollama partners with OpenAI to bring gpt-oss to Ollama and its community.

items.title: Ollama's new app
items.description: Ollama's new app is now available for macOS and Windows.

items.title: Secure Minions: private collaboration between Ollama and frontier models
items.description: Secure Minions is a secure protocol built by Stanford's Hazy Research lab to allow encrypted local-remote communication.

items.title: Thinking
items.description: Ollama now has the ability to enable or disable thinking. This gives users the flexibility to choose the model’s thinking behavior for different applications and use cases.

items.title: Streaming responses with tool calling
items.description: Ollama now supports streaming responses with tool calling. This enables all chat applications to stream content and also call tools in real time.

items.title: Ollama's new engine for multimodal models
items.description: Ollama now supports new multimodal models with its new engine.

items.title: Minions: where local and cloud LLMs meet
items.description: Avanika Narayan, Dan Biderman, and Sabri Eyuboglu from Christopher Ré's Stanford Hazy Research lab, along with Avner May, Scott Linderman, James Zou, have developed a way to shift a substantial portion of LLM workloads to consumer devices by having small on-device models (such as Llama 3.2 with Ollama) collaborate with larger models in the cloud (such as GPT-4o).

items.title: Structured outputs
items.description: Ollama now supports structured outputs making it possible to constrain a model's output to a specific format defined by a JSON schema. The Ollama Python and JavaScript libraries have been updated to support structured outputs.

items.title: Ollama Python library 0.4 with function calling improvements
items.description: With Ollama Python library version 0.4, functions can now be provided as tools. The library now also has full typing support and new examples have been added.

items.title: Llama 3.2 Vision
items.description: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B and 90B models are now available in Ollama.

items.title: IBM Granite 3.0 models
items.description: Ollama partners with IBM to bring Granite 3.0 models to Ollama.

items.title: Llama 3.2 goes small and multimodal
items.description: Ollama partners with Meta to bring Llama 3.2 to Ollama.

items.title: Reduce hallucinations with Bespoke-Minicheck
items.description: Bespoke-Minicheck is a new grounded factuality checking model developed by Bespoke Labs that is now available in Ollama. It can fact-check responses generated by other models to detect and reduce hallucinations.

items.title: Tool support
items.description: Ollama now supports tool calling with popular models such as Llama 3.1. This enables a model to answer a given prompt using tool(s) it knows about, making it possible for models to perform more complex tasks or interact with the outside world.

items.title: Google Gemma 2
items.description: Gemma 2 is now available on Ollama in 3 sizes - 2B, 9B and 27B.

items.title: An entirely open-source AI code assistant inside your editor
items.description: Continue enables you to easily create your own coding assistant directly inside Visual Studio Code and JetBrains with open-source LLMs.

items.title: Google announces Firebase Genkit with Ollama support
items.description: At Google IO 2024, Google announced Ollama support in Firebase Genkit, a new open-source framework for developers to build, deploy and monitor production-ready AI-powered apps.

items.title: Llama 3 is not very censored
items.description: Compared to Llama 2, Llama 3 feels much less censored. Meta has substantially lowered false refusal rates. Llama 3 will refuse less than 1/3 of the prompts previously refused by Llama 2.

items.title: Llama 3
items.description: Llama 3 is now available to run on Ollama. This model is the next generation of Meta's state-of-the-art large language model, and is the most capable openly available LLM to date.

items.title: Embedding models
items.description: Embedding models are available in Ollama, making it easy to generate vector embeddings for use in search and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) applications.

items.title: Ollama now supports AMD graphics cards
items.description: Ollama now supports AMD graphics cards in preview on Windows and Linux. All the features of Ollama can now be accelerated by AMD graphics cards on Ollama for Linux and Windows.

items.title: Windows preview
items.description: Ollama is now available on Windows in preview, making it possible to pull, run and create large language models in a new native Windows experience. Ollama on Windows includes built-in GPU acceleration, access to the full model library, and serves the Ollama API including OpenAI compatibility.

items.title: OpenAI compatibility
items.description: Ollama now has initial compatibility with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, making it possible to use existing tooling built for OpenAI with local models via Ollama.

items.title: Vision models
items.description: New vision models are now available: LLaVA 1.6, in 7B, 13B and 34B parameter sizes. These models support higher resolution images, improved text recognition and logical reasoning.

items.title: Python & JavaScript Libraries
items.description: The initial versions of the Ollama Python and JavaScript libraries are now available, making it easy to integrate your Python or JavaScript, or Typescript app with Ollama in a few lines of code. Both libraries include all the features of the Ollama REST API, are familiar in design, and compatible with new and previous versions of Ollama.

items.title: Building LLM-Powered Web Apps with Client-Side Technology
items.description: Recreate one of the most popular LangChain use-cases with open source, locally running software - a chain that performs Retrieval-Augmented Generation, or RAG for short, and allows you to “chat with your documents”

items.title: Ollama is now available as an official Docker image
items.description: Ollama can now run with Docker Desktop on the Mac, and run inside Docker containers with GPU acceleration on Linux.

items.title: Leveraging LLMs in your Obsidian Notes
items.description: This post walks through how you could incorporate a local LLM using Ollama in Obsidian, or potentially any note taking tool.

items.title: How to prompt Code Llama
items.description: This guide walks through the different ways to structure prompts for Code Llama and its different variations and features including instructions, code completion and fill-in-the-middle (FIM).

items.title: Run Code Llama locally
items.description: Meta's Code Llama is now available on Ollama to try.

items.title: Run Llama 2 uncensored locally
items.description: This post will give some example comparisons running Llama 2 uncensored model versus its censored model.

Introducing Adaptive: a smarter way to use Windsurf
items.description: We're launching multiple updates to Windsurf today: an Adaptive model router, a redesigned model picker with pricing context, and the removal of daily limits for Max.

items.title: Introducing our new Windsurf pricing plans
items.description: We're simplifying Windsurf pricing across Free, Pro, and Teams alongside launching a new Max plan for our power users. The new plans replace the current credit-based system with industry-standard quotas.

items.title: GPT-5.4 is now available in Windsurf
items.description: GPT-5.4 is now available in Windsurf with multiple reasoning effort levels. For a limited time, self serve users enjoy promotional pricing starting at 1x credits.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in Windsurf
items.description: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in Windsurf with Low and High thinking variants. For a limited time, enjoy promotional pricing on credit usage.

items.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available in Windsurf
items.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available in Windsurf with limited-time promotional pricing for self serve users: 2x credits without thinking and 3x credits with thinking.

items.title: GLM-5 and Minimax M2.5 are now available in Windsurf
items.description: GLM-5 from Zhipu AI and Minimax M2.5 are now available in Windsurf with limited-time promotional pricing. Both models are included in Arena Mode's Frontier Arena and Hybrid Arena battle groups.

items.title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark is now available in Windsurf
items.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, an ultra-fast model optimized for real-time coding, is now available in Windsurf's Arena Mode Fast and Hybrid battle groups.

items.title: 1.9544.24 — Wave 14: Arena Mode
items.description: Arena Mode brings side-by-side model comparison directly into your IDE, plus Plan Mode for smarter task planning.

items.title: 1.13.8 — GPT-5.2-Codex
items.description: Adds support for GPT-5.2-Codex with four reasoning efforts (low, medium, high, and xhigh). GPT-5.2-Codex is OpenAI's latest model designed for agentic coding. It excels at working in large codebases over long sessions. For most tasks, we recommend using the medium reasoning effort.

items.title: 1.13.5 — Gemini 3 Flash
items.description: Gemini 3 Flash is now available for all users. This model combines Gemini 3 Pro-grade reasoning with Flash-level speed and efficiency, making it ideal for agentic workflows and coding tasks.

items.title: 1.13.3 — Wave 13: Merry Shipmas
items.description: Wave 13 brings first-class support for parallel, multi-agent sessions in Windsurf, along with Git worktrees, side-by-side Cascade panes, and a dedicated terminal profile for more reliable agent execution.

items.title: Making Dagster Easier to Contribute to in an AI-Driven World
items.description: AI has made contributing to open source easier but reviewing contributions is still hard. At Dagster, we’re improving the contributor experience with smarter review tooling, clearer guidelines, and a focus on contributions that are easier to evaluate, merge, and maintain.

items.title: DataOps with Dagster: A Practical Guide to Building a Reliable Data Platform
items.description: DataOps is about building a system that provides visibility into what's happening and control over how it behaves

items.title: Unlocking the Full Value of Your Databricks
items.description: Standardizing on Databricks is a smart strategic move, but consolidation alone does not create a working operating model across teams, tools, and downstream systems. By pairing Databricks and Unity Catalog with Dagster, enterprises can add the coordination layer needed for dependency visibility, end-to-end lineage, and faster, more confident delivery at scale.

items.title: Announcing AI Driven Data Engineering
items.description: AI coding agents are changing how data engineers work. This Dagster University course shows how to build a production-ready ELT pipeline from prompts while learning practical patterns for reliable AI-assisted development.

items.title: When to Move from Dagster OSS to Dagster+
items.description: Dagster OSS is built for builders. But as teams grow, the operational burden of running the platform can quietly consume engineering time. This guide explains when it makes sense to move to Dagster+ and shift your focus back to building data products.

items.title: Sample-Level Versioning for ML Pipelines with Dagster and Metaxy
items.description: Learn how Metaxy can be used to build multimodal data pipelines with sample-level granularity on Dagster

items.title: Evaluating Skills
items.description: We built a light weight evaluation framework to quantiatively measure the effectiveness of the Dagster Skills, and these are our findings.

items.title: Great Infrastructure Needs Great Stories: Designing our Children’s Book
items.description: We set out to explain Dagster assets in the simplest possible way: as living characters that wait, react, and change with their dependencies. By designing a children’s book with warmth, visuals, and motion, we rediscovered what makes assets compelling in the first place.

items.title: Closing the DataOps Loop: Why We Built Compass for Dagster+
items.description: Detection isn't the bottleneck anymore. Understanding is. Compass closes the loop by turning Dagster+ operational data into a conversation.

items.title: Pytest for Agent-Generated Code: Concrete Testing Strategies to Put Into Practice
items.description: When agents write tests, intent matters as much as correctness. By defining clear testing levels, preferred patterns, and explicit anti-patterns, we give agents the structure they need to produce fast, reliable Pytest suites that scale with automation.

items.title: Dagster + Snowflake: Building Production AI Pipelines with Cortex
items.description: Snowflake handles AI compute while Dagster handles orchestration, observability, and the operational patterns that turn AI experiments into reliable production pipelines.

items.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.6
items.description: We’re upgrading our smartest model. Across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance, Opus 4.6 is an industry-leading model, often by wide margin.

items.title: Claude is a space to think
items.description: We’ve made a choice: Claude will remain ad-free. We explain why advertising incentives are incompatible with a genuinely helpful AI assistant, and how we plan to expand access without compromising user trust.

items.description: Infrastructure configuration can swing agentic coding benchmarks by several percentage points—sometimes more than the leaderboard gap between top models.

items.title: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands
Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
items.description: All modern language models sometimes act like they have emotions. What’s behind these behaviors? Our interpretability team investigates.

Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves
